examples.title: Array Declaration and Access in C
examples.description: Basic array operations showing declaration, initialization, and element access
examples.explanation: Line 5: Declares array of 5 integers and initializes with values. Line 8-9: Direct access using index (0-based). Line 12: Modifying element at index 1. Line 16-18: Loop through all elements using index.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for access, O(n) for traversal

examples.title: Array/List Operations in Python
examples.description: Python list operations demonstrating array-like behavior
examples.code:
# Python uses lists (dynamic arrays)
arr = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]

# Accessing elements
print(f"First element: {arr[0]}")   # Output: 10
print(f"Last element: {arr[-1]}")   # Output: 50 (negative indexing)

# Modifying elements
arr[1] = 25
print(f"Modified: {arr}")  # Output: [10, 25, 30, 40, 50]

# Traversing
for i, element in enumerate(arr):
    print(f"Index {i}: {element}")

# Length of array
print(f"Size: {len(arr)}")  # Output: 5

# Using NumPy for true arrays
import numpy as np
np_arr = np.array([10, 20, 30, 40, 50])
print(f"NumPy array: {np_arr}")
examples.explanation: Python lists are dynamic arrays. Line 5-6: Access using positive and negative indices. Line 12-13: enumerate() gives both index and value. Line 19-21: NumPy provides true fixed-size arrays.

examples.title: Array Operations in Java
examples.description: Java array declaration, initialization, and traversal
examples.explanation: Line 4: Array literal initialization. Line 7: Creates array with default values (0 for int). Line 11: .length property gives size. Line 18-20: Enhanced for-loop for traversal. Line 24-26: Traditional indexed loop.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for access, O(n) for traversal
